title: K5: action_hint with legal example on scope-regex validation failure
description: When the `audience` field (fab_propose) fails SCOPE_COORDINATE_PATTERN (/^[a-z0-9_-]+(:[a-z0-9_-]+)*$/u) validation, the tool currently bare re-throws a generic zod message with no example of a legal value. Upgrade the error reply to carry a structured `action_hint` that includes a concrete legal example string (e.g. `project:fabric-v2`). REUSE the existing payload-warning.ts action_hint vocabulary (StructuredToolWarning.action_hint) — do NOT create a parallel error-hint system.
action: In extract-knowledge.ts (fab_propose handler, around the `FabExtractKnowledgeInputSchema.parse(input)` at line 49), catch the ZodError, detect when an issue path includes `audience` with `code === 'invalid_string'`/regex failure, and rethrow/return a structured error carrying `action_hint` reusing the StructuredToolWarning shape from payload-warning.ts. The action_hint MUST contain a legal example, exact string: `audience must be a scope coordinate like "project:fabric-v2", "team", or "personal" (lowercase, segments joined by ':')`. Apply the SAME zod regex hint via a shared `describe`/refinement message at the schema level (api-contracts.ts:701-704 audience field) so the example is the single source of truth: change `.regex(SCOPE_COORDINATE_PATTERN)` to `.regex(SCOPE_COORDINATE_PATTERN, { message: 'audience must be a scope coordinate like "project:fabric-v2", "team", or "personal"' })`. Then surface that message into the structured action_hint in the handler. Do NOT invent new warning codes — use the existing StructuredToolWarning {code, file, message, action_hint} shape; code can be a stable literal like `scope_coordinate_invalid`.

criteria: RED first: a test in extract-knowledge.test.ts calls fab_propose with an illegal audience (e.g. `Project:Bad Caps`) and asserts the error/response carries an `action_hint` string containing `project:fabric-v2`; it fails before the impl change.
grep-verifiable: `grep -n 'project:fabric-v2' packages/shared/src/schemas/api-contracts.ts` matches (the regex message carries the legal example).
grep-verifiable: `grep -n 'action_hint' packages/server/src/tools/extract-knowledge.ts` matches (handler surfaces a structured action_hint on validation failure).
grep-verifiable: `grep -n 'SCOPE_COORDINATE_PATTERN, {' packages/shared/src/schemas/api-contracts.ts` matches (regex now carries a custom message, not the generic zod default).

Reuse check: the action_hint uses the StructuredToolWarning shape from payload-warning.ts — `grep -n 'StructuredToolWarning\|action_hint' packages/server/src/tools/extract-knowledge.ts` shows the existing helper/type imported, not a new parallel interface.
Schema-edit gate: `pnpm --filter @fenglimg/fabric-shared build` was run (dist rebuilt) BEFORE server vitest, because api-contracts.ts changed.
convergence.verification: cd /Users/wepie/Desktop/personal-projects/pcf && pnpm --filter @fenglimg/fabric-shared build && grep -n 'project:fabric-v2' packages/shared/src/schemas/api-contracts.ts && grep -n 'action_hint' packages/server/src/tools/extract-knowledge.ts && pnpm -r exec tsc --noEmit && pnpm --filter @fenglimg/fabric-server exec vitest run
convergence.definition_of_done: A caller who passes an illegal audience scope coordinate gets back a structured error whose action_hint shows a legal example (project:fabric-v2), so the agent can self-correct without guessing the regex.

files.change: Add custom message to `.regex(SCOPE_COORDINATE_PATTERN, { message: '...project:fabric-v2...' })` so the legal example is the single source of truth.
Catch ZodError, detect audience regex failure, return/throw a structured error carrying action_hint (StructuredToolWarning shape) with the legal example.
Add RED test: illegal audience -> error carries action_hint containing project:fabric-v2.

implementation: RED: add test in extract-knowledge.test.ts calling fab_propose with audience='Project:Bad Caps' (uppercase + space = regex fail); assert the thrown/returned error exposes action_hint containing 'project:fabric-v2'. Confirm it fails.

GREEN schema: in api-contracts.ts:703 change `.regex(SCOPE_COORDINATE_PATTERN)` to `.regex(SCOPE_COORDINATE_PATTERN, { message: 'audience must be a scope coordinate like "project:fabric-v2", "team", or "personal"' })`.
Schema-edit gate: run `pnpm --filter @fenglimg/fabric-shared build` to rebuild dist (else runtime invalid_union_discriminator).

GREEN handler: wrap `FabExtractKnowledgeInputSchema.parse(input)` in try/catch; on ZodError with an issue at path including 'audience', build a structured error using the StructuredToolWarning shape (import type from payload-warning.ts) with code='scope_coordinate_invalid', message=zod issue message, action_hint=the legal-example string; throw/return it.
REFACTOR: ensure the example string is sourced from the schema message (no second copy drifting); confirm no new warning interface was introduced.

success_metrics: RED test failed before impl, green after
reuses StructuredToolWarning shape, no parallel system
shared dist rebuilt before server tests

reference.pattern: payload-warning.ts StructuredToolWarning {code,file,message,action_hint} — reuse for the error envelope

rationale.chosen_approach: Single source of truth for the legal example at the zod regex message, surfaced through the existing StructuredToolWarning shape, to avoid a parallel error-hint vocabulary.
decision_factors: C-004 mandate to include a legal example
rationale.tradeoffs: Touching api-contracts.ts forces the shared-build gate; acceptable since K5 must improve the schema-level message.

risks: Forgetting `pnpm --filter @fenglimg/fabric-shared build` after the schema edit -> server tests hit stale dist / invalid_union_discriminator.

fab_propose currently re-parses via FabExtractKnowledgeInputSchema.parse (line 49) AND registerTool validates against FabExtractKnowledgeInputShape — ensure the action_hint fires on the regex failure regardless of which layer rejects it.
